The initial steps involved linking obvious pairs - for example, Emma has a dog, drinks tea, and works as a doctor. More complex clues connected seemingly unrelated attributes; for example,  "Tea -> Pasta" which meant that anyone who drinks tea *must* also eat pasta. I began to build profiles for each individual, constantly updating them as new pieces of information emerged.

At the core of my approach was the meticulous elimination process. If a particular attribute was already linked to someone, it removed that attribute as a possibility for anyone else. I grouped attributes into categories: Emma drinks Tea, eats Pasta, drives Subway, lives in Yellow, has the Dog, and is a Doctor.
